faqs.a: No. Perfectionism isn't a clinical diagnosis. It's a personality pattern. It can show up alongside conditions like anxiety, depression, or obsessive-compulsive disorder, but the word itself describes a tendency, not a disorder.

faqs.q: What's the difference between perfectionism and high standards?
faqs.a: High standards are flexible and can be energizing, and a person can do excellent work without punishing themselves over every flaw. Perfectionism tends to be rigid and draining, treating anything short of flawless as failure and tying self-worth to performance.

It can. When standards stop being a guide and start being a stick, perfectionism can fuel anxiety, stall projects before they begin, and lead to burnout. If the inner critic rarely goes quiet, a therapist can help loosen the link between worth and performance.
